description_long: This is a small, vintage-style biplane with a striking blue and white color scheme. The aircraft features two stacked wings connected by struts and wires, with the upper wing slightly larger than the lower. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, which displays a star insignia on the side. The nose houses a single propeller, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The cockpit is positioned just behind the upper wing, likely open and designed for a single pilot. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted with matching blue and white patterns.
description: A small, blue and white vintage biplane with dual wings, a single propeller, and an open cockpit. The design features bold geometric patterns and a star insignia on the fuselage.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked wings, central propeller, and main landing gear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the star insignia, tail structure, and the open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, vertical stabilizer, and the alignment of the wings and landing gear.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the propeller, cockpit, and the geometric wing and tail patterns.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, staggered wings connected by struts and wires; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a single tailwheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing.
